However, since the hint suggests clicking the back arrow, it implies moving out of the current book view might be necessary before accessing the library.

(Screenshot Analysis)
The screenshot shows a book page with a back arrow at the top left corner, which is typically used to return to the previous screen or the main library view. The presence of the back arrow suggests a way to navigate away from the current book details.

(Next Action)
Click the back arrow icon at the top left corner to navigate back to the previous screen or the main library view.

(Grounded Action)
```python
agent.click("Back arrow icon", 1)
```

(Previous action verification)
The current screenshot shows the menu options for a book in Google Play Books, but there is no indication that the library has been entered. The task requires navigating to the library section, which might involve closing this menu and finding the appropriate navigation option.

(Screenshot Analysis)
The menu includes options like "About this book," "Share," "Add bookmark," etc., but none directly relate to entering the library. The back arrow suggests a way to exit the current view and potentially find the library icon or tab.

(Next Action)
Close the current menu and look for a library icon or tab at the top of the screen.

(Grounded Action)
```python

```
